nts1.d: International party starters from Huntleys + Palmers label head.
Hip-hop scholar, esteemed illustrator and all round head Ted Draws holds down a killer 2 hour monthly slot on Thursday afternoons.

nts1.n: THE TIM & BARRY SHOW w/ Tim b2b DJ spinn & Traxman
nts1.d:
Tim & Barry have been documenting underground music since 2000. Journey from the latest UK drill back in time to classic grime and garage, all the way to afrobeats, dancehall and RnB. Expect the unexpected.
From the host: "In this special mix recorded live on Tim's Farm,  Tim Goes B2B with 2 Chicago legends for 3 sets firstly footwork vs Dennery segment with DJ spinn for the first 40 mins a theme continued in the second B2B with Traxman. The final 40 mins is a multigenre excursion with the final B2B with Corky Strong."

nts1.n: RADIO SUPER W/ ROSA PISTOLA - Tributo a EROS
nts1.d:
RADIO SUPER is Rosa Pistola’s show on NTS where she dives deep into the Latin American underground. Each episode zooms into different genres, scenes, and vibes, tracing their roots and evolution. It’s all about the sounds, the stories, and the pillars of the underground.
From the host: "This mixtape is a tribute to Eros. Eros is a young Colombian producer who specializes in BALA, a subgenre of guaracha with a denser, darker sound."

In an era of everything at once, Gabrielle Kwarteng is a DJ who weaves stories that often look to the past, while always focusing on possible futures. Dip in to hear a range of dance music from downtempo to deep house to techno rollers - and everything in between.

MOBBS chopping up whatever he finds 
ohm.mobbs@protonmail.com

Cutting edge East African club sounds from Kampala's finest party starter.
From the host: "Masaka Masaka is a Ugandan Producer, DJ and Artist, his work features heavily mangled sound designs, percussion, Jazz influence, deconstructed sonics juxtaposed with chopped vocals and occasional basslines; His EP "Barely Making Much was released on Hakuna Kulala"
Classic Jamaican records from the "Rocksteady Daddy" Asher G.
London & Istanbul based record label Zel Zele takes us on a monthly ride to earthly wonders and cosmic adventures. Bite the lip take the trip!
Organic soundscapes, field recordings and melodic ambience from composer Sophia Zhuravkova.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO
nts1.d: First thing on Fridays - Jack Rollo's here to take care of things.

nts1.n: The NTS Breakfast Show w/ TASH LC
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SCARY THINGS
UP YA ARCHIVES w/ Chinese Daughter

nts2.d: Regular hours from the D.C. archivists and record label, famed for uncovering + documenting folk and global traditional music.

Where the wall meets the floor, Temporal Cove is a body of sound exploring the ethereal and ephemeral. Smilegoth dives into shoegaze, post-rock, house and more to articulate a flow of reverberations that can be felt on every surface.
From the host: "smilegoth invites one of her favorite bands, Model/Actriz for a 1 hour guest mix on Temporal Cove. Ruben and Jack from Model/Actriz dig through their favorite live(ish) drum recordings."

Brian Weitz has been making music as Geologist with Animal Collective for going on two decades. The egg first cracked in the mid 90's at Baltimore record fairs and long overnight radio shows in New York on WKCR. Whether making sounds in the practice space or playing records, his bandmates call it the O'Brien System. One Tuesday each month you can go there with him on NTS 2.
From the host: _"Sounds from recent life. Croz Boyce, west coast tour with Bitchin Bajas, DPSD, Dean Spunt, Cool Maritime, Cecyl Ruehlen. Complex Crown mia on the internet. Some car listens as well." _

Forager Records is a Los Angeles based record label, committed to unearthing and breathing new life into rare and under appreciated sounds from the past.
From the host: "An hour long mix of deep South African tunes from guest host Biko for a Soundbath Records takeover."

nts2.n: Brown Acid Trippin' w/ Lance Barresi & DJ Tara Dactyl
nts2.d:
Life is short, have more fun. Lance Barresi of Permanent Records goes for all vinyl 45's, live and direct from the Los Angeles studio.
From the host: "An alphabetical journey through the Brown Acid Archives. All of these tracks come from real, vintage vinyl 45s from Hard Rock, Heavy Psych, and Proto-Metal artists beginning with the letter P!”

An auditory exploration with SF native Mishka ranging from synth pop and balearic to softrock and global dance gems. One hour, once a month, with love.
From the host: "OHYUNG is the solo project of musician and film composer Lia Ouyang Rusli. She brings together disparate sounds and influences in her music, drawing on ambient soundscapes, harsh noise and euphoric pop into a singular sound. Her newest record IOWA is an atmospheric ode to the vast beauty of the Midwest, and was described by Pitchfork as a "chilling, starkly beautiful ambient album." As a film composer she recently scored A24’s Sorry Baby (dir. Eva Victor), Happyend (dir. Neo Sora), A24's Problemista and HBO's Fantasmas (dir. Julio Torres)"
Radio live transmissions from the multidisciplinary and independent, deadAir & co. Come get shown the light!
Adam Cooper aka Foreigner heads the Foreign Hour show - a monthly offering of contemporary music from dembow to hip-hop shining light on the diaspora.

nts2.n: CDMX Tapes w/ BRVVS & BASY TROPIKALNE
nts2.d: Mexican record label, Naafi, settle in at NTS for a showcase of the weirdest experimentations in club culture that have bubbled to the surface of Soundcloud. Dazed rhythms converging into Jersey stutters, and tumblr-era trap for two straight hours on a Monday.

DJ, artist and techno impresario, Word of Command welds fevered collage and high-octane techno into a hallucinatory dreamscape. He is the founder of FIST (@funisstilltransgressive), a queer techno party that pays tribute to radical fun, while engaging the visibility of communities of otherness through techno in NYC.
ZULI, experimental club producer and VENT co-founder hailing from Cairo hits the NTS airwaves to showcase his club wares: aggressively left-field.
Shanghai, China collective 626 Company, headed up by Jackzebra deliver monthly broadcasts from the collective and their friends.

nts2.n: CONFUSING MIX W/ JOSH DA COSTA
Improvisational Aesthetics w/ LAGOS (Leo123 x RADIUS)
Sacred Space: Spring Special w/ Radha

subtle.d: Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: George Jensen and Heavy Hymns - Quietudes
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
In Sheep's Clothing – World of Sound
Dustin Wong – Why Did Why Did Why Did

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

rinse.n: Lupe Fuentes presents In The Loop

rinsefr.j: Les femmes noirs dans l’industrie de la musique: Héritages, vols et résistance
DJ Monique présente Les boss Lady de la musique
International Women's Day Takeover with Shinobihana, Malaureins, Izis, 3msi, Sugar Tantine & 3msi
#25 La représentation des FLINTA dans les musiques électroniques
